sections.paragraph:
σχολάζω (σχολή) 1 aor. ἐσχόλασα (Aeschyl., Thu. et al.; ins, pap, LXX; TestSol 1:10 L; TestJob 6:3, 7; TestJud 20:1; Philo; in gener. of release from routine or pressing obligation; wars, e.g., permit no leisure Pla., Leg. 694e)

① to have time or leisure for, busy oneself with, devote oneself to, give one’s time to τινί someone or someth. (Lucian, Ver. Hist. 2, 15; Ps.-Lucian, Macrob. 4 φιλοσοφίᾳ; Epict. 2, 14, 28; Herodian 1, 8, 1 al.; SIG 717, 34f [100 b.c.] τοῖς φιλοσόφοις; OGI 569, 23 θεῶν θρησκείᾳ; Sb 4284, 15 τῇ γῇ; PAmh 82, 6 γεωργίᾳ; Philo, Spec. Leg. 3, 1 al.; TestJud 20:1) τῇ προσευχῇ 1 Cor 7:5 (on this subj. s. TestNapht 8:8); cp. IPol 1:3. Χριστιανὸς θεῷ σχολάζει 7:3 (cp. the letter by a polytheist Sb 4515 οὐ μέλλω θεῷ σχολάζειν, εἰ μὴ πρότερον ἀπαρτίσω τὸν υἱόν μου ‘I don’t plan on having time for a deity until I first educate my son’).

② to be without occupants, be unoccupied, stand empty (Plut., C. Gracch. 840 [12, 6] τόπος; Julian, Caes. 316c καθέδρα; SEG XI, 121, 13) of a house Mt 12:44; Lk 11:25 v.l. HNyberg, Ntl. Sem. zu Uppsala 4, ’36, 22–35, ConNeot 13, ’49, 1–11.—DELG s.v. σχολή. M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

gener. gener. = generally

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

Ps.-Lucian Ps.-Lucian , after II a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

PAmh PAmh = The Amherst Papyri I–II—List 4

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

TestNapht TestNapht = Testament of Naphthali, s. Test12Patr—List 2

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Julian Julian , various works; IV a.d.—List 5

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

ConNeot ConNeot = Coniectanea Neotestamentica—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 982
